100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On April 3, 1926, a significant political shift unfolded as the British Parliament passed the Unemployment Insurance Bill, aimed at providing financial relief to the rising number of jobless citizens in the wake of post-war economic struggles. In the realm of international relations, tensions escalated as Italy, under Mussolini, tightened its grip on power, prompting concerns among European nations about the spread of fascism. Meanwhile, in the United States, the ongoing debate over Prohibition continued to stir public sentiment, with calls for repeal gaining momentum.
Updated: Friday, April 3, 2026 at 07:32:25 AM CDT
